---
title: Network visualization
jupyter: python3
---

### Set up and load data

```{python}

## Import modules
import abct
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from scipy import io

## Load data
data = io.loadmat("./data/hcp_data.mat")

```

### Get structural and correlation networks

```{python}

## Get structural networks
W = data["W"]

WD = dict()
WD["Original"] = W
WD["Degree-corrected"] = abct.residualn(W, "degree")
WD["Co-neighbor"] = abct.kneighbors(W, "common", kappa=0.1).todense()

## Get correlation networks
X = data["X"]
C = X.T @ X

CD = dict()
CD["Original"] = C
CD["Degree-corrected"] = abct.residualn(C, "degree")
CD["Co-neighbor"] = abct.kneighbors(C, "common", kappa=0.1).todense()

```

### Visualize structural networks

```{python}

ordw = data["ordw"].flatten() - 1
zmin = (0, -0.005, 0)
zmax = (0.01, 0.005, 36)
for i, name in enumerate(WD):
    fig = px.imshow(
        WD[name][np.ix_(ordw, ordw)], 
        title=f"{name} structural network",
        color_continuous_scale="inferno",
        zmin=zmin[i], zmax=zmax[i],
        width=600, height=600)
    fig.update_layout(title_x=0.5).show()

```

### Visualize correlation networks

```{python}

ordc = data["ordc"].flatten() - 1
zmin = (0, -0.2, 0)
zmax = (1, 0.2, 36)
for i, name in enumerate(CD):
    fig = px.imshow(
        CD[name][np.ix_(ordc, ordc)], 
        title=f"{name} correlation network",
        color_continuous_scale="viridis",
        zmin=zmin[i], zmax=zmax[i],
        width=600, height=600)
    fig.update_layout(title_x=0.5).show()

```
